name: Rauw Alejandro
description:
'''Raúl Alejandro Ocasio Ruiz''' (born January 10, 1993), known professionally as '''Rauw Alejandro''', is a Puerto Rican singer, songwriter, and dancer. Known for his versatility and innovative blend of [[reggaeton]] with [[Pop music|pop]], [[Contemporary R&B|R&B]], [[Electronic music|electronic music]], and traditional [[Caribbean music|Caribbean]] styles, he is one of the leading figures in contemporary Latin urban music. His accolades include two [[Latin Grammy Awards]], two [[Billboard Latin Music Awards]], and five [[Grammy Award]] nominations. Born and raised in [[San Juan, Puerto Rico|San Juan]] and [[Carolina, Puerto Rico|Carolina]], Puerto Rico, Alejandro was introduced to music at an early age by his guitarist father and backing vocalist mother, who exposed him to artists like [[Elvis Presley]] and [[Michael Jackson]]. He initially pursued a career in [[Association football|soccer]], playing for the Puerto Rican national team and receiving a sports scholarship, before an injury led him to focus on music full-time. He began uploading songs to [[SoundCloud]] in 2014 and signed with Duars Entertainment in 2017. His breakthrough came with the 2018 remix of "[[Toda (song)|Toda]]" featuring [[Cazzu]], [[Lenny Tavárez]], and [[Lyanno]], which became a major streaming hit and introduced him to a wider Latin American audience. He followed with collaborations with artists like [[Ozuna]], [[Nicky Jam]], and [[Chencho Corleone]], which further solidified his presence on urban music charts.

Alejandro's debut studio album, ''[[Afrodisíaco (album)|Afrodisíaco]]'' (2020), earned him his first Grammy nomination for [[Grammy Award for Best Música Urbana Album|Best Música Urbana Album]]. He achieved mainstream commercial success with his second album, ''[[Vice Versa (Rauw Alejandro album)|Vice Versa]]'' (2021), which debuted at number one on ''Billboard''{{'}}s [[Top Latin Albums]] chart and spawned the global hit "[[Todo de Ti]]". The song became his most commercially successful release, reaching number three on the [[Billboard Global 200|''Billboard'' Global 200]] chart and receiving nominations for [[Latin Grammy Award for Record of the Year|Record of the Year]] and [[Latin Grammy Award for Song of the Year|Song of the Year]]. His third album, ''[[Saturno (album)|Saturno]]'' (2022), marked a stylistic shift toward electronic and synth-driven sounds, received a Grammy nomination, and was supported by the Saturno World Tour, which grossed over $50 million and sold over 550,000 tickets across North America and Europe. In 2023, he released the surprise spin-off ''[[Playa Saturno]]'' and the collaborative EP ''[[RR (EP)|RR]]'' with [[Rosalía]], which confirmed their engagement and debuted at number two on the Hot Latin Songs chart with its lead single "[[Beso (song)|Beso]]".

Alejandro's fifth studio album, ''[[Cosa Nuestra]]'' (2024), marked a critical and commercial peak, debuting at number one on the Top Latin Albums chart and number six on the [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]], his highest-charting position on the all-genre chart. The album's 18 tracks all charted on the Hot Latin Songs chart, with five debuting in the top ten. The album received nominations for [[Latin Grammy Award for Album of the Year]] and [[Grammy Award for Best Latin Pop Album]]. Its theatrical supporting tour grossed over $91 million, becoming the most lucrative tour of his career and the second-highest-grossing Latin tour of 2025. He followed with the prequel album ''[[Cosa Nuestra: Capítulo 0]]'' (2025), which explored his Caribbean musical roots through salsa, bachata, plena, and bomba, debuting at number three on the Top Latin Albums chart and marking his eighth consecutive top-ten entry. He has received a [[Hispanic Heritage Awards|Hispanic Heritage Award]] for Vision, recognizing his contributions to Latin music and his role in shaping its global future.
